title: 1  Parallel Computing
1  Parallel Computing
text:
1.3 Question 3

true_mean = 1

coverage <- replicate(1000, {
boot_s <- rexp(50)
boot_means <- replicate(1000, 
  mean(sample(boot_s, 50, replace = TRUE)))

CI <- quantile(boot_means, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))

(true_mean >= CI[1]) & (true_mean <= CI[2])} )

mean(coverage)

[1] 0.948

title: 1  Parallel Computing
1  Parallel Computing
text:
1.5 Question 5
Using the parLapply() to solve Q4

cl <- makeCluster(3)

system.time({
c <- parLapply(cl, 1:3 , function(a){
  x <- rnorm(5)
  max(x)})
})

   user  system elapsed 
   0.00    0.00    0.02 

stopCluster(cl)
c

[[1]]
[1] 1.228088

[[2]]
[1] 0.2688596

[[3]]
[1] 1.841409


Use replicate()

system.time({
max_values <- replicate(3, max(rnorm(5)))
})

   user  system elapsed 
      0       0       0 

max_values

[1] 2.4158352 0.4595894 0.5747557


3 different methods were used to complete question 4 and compare the times taken. Parallel computing method - parLappy() recorded the highest time, followed by foreach() and lastly replicate().
